title: Fazah — Decentralized Pilgrim Relief Network
description: Every pilgrim carries a quick response (QR) card that connects them to the nearest guide or hotel reception in moments, without needing a phone or internet on their part.
demoNotice: This is an explanatory page for the proposed service concept. The displayed details (pilgrim card and alert simulator) are fully experimental for explanation purposes only.

why.title: When a pilgrim needs help, there is no time to waste
why.desc: In the crowd of the Two Holy Mosques, a pilgrim may get lost or experience a health problem away from their group. Fazah is a volunteer network combining certified guides and hotel receptionists, so that any of them can identify any pilgrim carrying the service card and alert their supervisors instantly.

how.title: How does the service work?
how.desc: Three simple steps connect the pilgrim to the relief network without complexity.

simulator.title: Scanning & Alert Simulation
simulator.idleDesc: Click the button below to simulate a guide scanning the Fazah card of the mock pilgrim above.
simulator.doneText: The group leader and hotel reception have been notified of the pilgrim's current location.

cardLabels.title: Fazah Card
cardLabels.qrDesc: Any registered guide or hotel receptionist scans this code to display the pilgrim's details and alert the relief network instantly.

sampleCard.name: Abdulrahman Saeed Al-Mutairi
sampleCard.healthNote: Hypertension patient, carries medicine in his bag

s1.title: Personal Fazah Card
s1.desc: Every pilgrim receives a card or wristband carrying a printed QR code, without needing a phone or internet. The card contains their name, hotel, room number, group leader's phone, blood type, and health notes.

s2.title: Scan by any Guide or Receptionist
s2.desc: When needed, the nearest certified guide or registered receptionist scans the QR code via their phone camera, immediately displaying the pilgrim's info without needing a special app.

s3.title: Instant Alert & Help Chain
s3.desc: Upon scanning, the network automatically sends an alert to the group leader and emergency contact, sharing the pilgrim's current location and notifying the hotel reception, starting the help chain without delay.

b1.title: No Phone or Internet Needed
b1.desc: The card works without any device on the pilgrim's side; simply carrying it as a card or wristband is enough.

b2.title: Distributed Volunteer Network
b2.desc: Any guide or receptionist registered in the network can assist the pilgrim wherever they are found.

b3.title: Critical Health Info When Needed
b3.desc: Blood type and health notes reach the first helper in a few seconds.

b4.title: Ideal for the Elderly & Disabled
b4.desc: A simple solution requiring no technical knowledge, suitable for all ages and conditions.
